message:
feat(ci): add checksum monitoring workflow

Create GitHub Action that:
- Runs twice daily (6 AM and 6 PM UTC)
- Verifies upstream installer checksums using security.sh --json
- Implements stability checking (2 matching runs before PR)
- Auto-creates PRs when checksums are stable

Closes: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-coj

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","title":"EPIC: acfs doctor & Update Commands","description":"# Epic: acfs doctor & Update Commands

## Overview
Build the diagnostic and update commands that help users verify and maintain their ACFS installation.

## Background & Reasoning
Beginners don't debug - they need clear "is it working?" feedback and simple fixes.

### acfs doctor
Single command to verify entire installation:
- Identity (user ubuntu, passwordless sudo)
- Shell setup (zsh, plugins, theme)
- All tools installed and working
- Clear PASS/FAIL/WARN for each check

### acfs update
Keep everything current:
- apt update + upgrade
- Update bun globals (codex, gemini, vercel, etc.)
- Optionally re-run tool installers

## Doctor Output Spec

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Dicklesworthstone stack
  PASS ✅ ntm (available)
  PASS ✅ slb (available)
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs structured data for website integration:
```json
{
  "checks": [
    {"id": "identity.user_is_ubuntu", "status": "pass", ...}
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] acfs doctor checks all ~40 tools
- [ ] Each check has stable ID for website mapping
- [ ] Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail
- [ ] --json mode for automation
- [ ] acfs update works without interaction
- [ ] Clear fix suggestions for each failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: 75r.1 (acfs doctor) and 75r.2 (acfs update). Both commands fully implemented with comprehensive options.","labels":["epic","tooling"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","title":"Implement acfs doctor command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs doctor Command

## Description
Build the diagnostic command that verifies the entire ACFS installation.

## Location
Install to: `~/.acfs/bin/acfs`
Subcommand: `acfs doctor`

## Output Format

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅
Workspace: /data/projects ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (zsh 5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## Check Structure

Each check has:
- id: Stable identifier for website mapping
- label: Human-readable name
- status: pass | warn | fail
- details: Version or extra info
- fix: Command to fix (on failure)

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash

run_check() {
    local id="$1"
    local label="$2"
    local check_cmd="$3"
    local fix_cmd="$4"
    
    if eval "$check_cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "PASS ✅ $label"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"pass\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
    else
        echo "FAIL ❌ $label"
        echo "      Fix: $fix_cmd"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"fail\",\"fix\":\"$fix_cmd\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
        ((FAIL_COUNT++))
    fi
}

# Run all checks
run_check "identity.user_is_ubuntu" "Logged in as ubuntu" \
    "[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]" \
    "sudo -u ubuntu bash"
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs:
```json
{
  "acfs_version": "0.1.0",
  "checks": [...],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: All pass (warnings OK)
- 1: Any failure
- 2: Doctor crashed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ~40 checks implemented
- [ ] Stable check IDs
- [ ] --json mode works
- [ ] Correct exit codes
- [ ] Fix suggestions on failure
- [ ] Summary at end","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:02.667771-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:02.667771-05:00","close_reason":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh exists with 332 lines implementing comprehensive health checks","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.268261-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.2","title":"Implement acfs update command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs update Command

## Description
Create command to update all ACFS-installed components.

## What It Updates

### System
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y && sudo apt autoremove -y
```

### Bun Globals
```bash
bun install -g @openai/codex@latest
bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest
bun install -g wrangler@latest
bun install -g supabase@latest
bun install -g vercel@latest
```

### Claude Code
```bash
claude update
```

### Dicklesworthstone Stack
- Check for updates to each tool
- Re-run installers if --force flag

## Flags
- --apt-only: Only system packages
- --agents-only: Only coding agents
- --stack-only: Only Dicklesworthstone tools
- --force: Re-run all installers even if up-to-date

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Updates apt packages
- [ ] Updates bun globals
- [ ] Updates Claude Code
- [ ] Optional stack update
- [ ] Progress output
- [ ] Summary of what was updated","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.55906-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:20:44.214258-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:20:44.214258-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented acfs update command with: apt updates, bun self-upgrade, agent updates (Claude/Codex/Gemini), cloud CLI updates (Wrangler/Supabase/Vercel), Rust/uv updates, optional stack updates. Supports --dry-run, --force, selective flags. Commit e6d310b.","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.560845-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7q1","title":"Wrap all phase functions with try_step calls","description":"# Task: Wrap all phase functions with try_step calls

## Context
Part of EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf)

## What to Do
Update all install_phase_* functions to use try_step() for each operation:

### Before
```bash
install_phase_languages() {
    curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
    curl -fsSL https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y
    # ...
}
```

### After
```bash
install_phase_languages() {
    CURRENT_PHASE_NAME="Language Runtimes"

    if ! command -v bun &>/dev/null; then
        try_step "Installing Bun runtime" \
            "acfs_curl https://bun.sh/install | bash" || return 1
    fi

    if ! command -v cargo &>/dev/null; then
        try_step "Installing Rust via rustup" \
            "acfs_curl https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y" || return 1
    fi
    # ...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All phases use try_step for each operation
- Each step has descriptive name
- Existing command skip logic preserved
- return 1 on any step failure

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: All install_phase_* functions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:02.642601-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:02.642601-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7q1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:31.195934-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7q1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:31.390618-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -81,10 +81,14 @@
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","title":"EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting","description":"# Epic: Per-Phase Error Reporting

## Overview
Replace generic 'installation failed' with structured error reports showing exactly which phase/step failed and how to fix it.

## Problem Statement
When installation fails, users see only 'ACFS installation failed' with no indication of which phase (1-10), what step, what the error was, or how to fix it.

## Business Impact
- Users can self-diagnose (reduces support burden)
- 'Phase 6' is actionable; 'failed' is not
- Most failures have known fixes (network, rate limits, deps)

## Technical Approach
1. Add CURRENT_PHASE, CURRENT_STEP, LAST_ERROR globals
2. Create try_step() wrapper for individual operations
3. Build ERROR_PATTERNS map with known fixes
4. Create report_failure() with structured output
5. Log JSON failure report for programmatic use

## Success Criteria
- Failure reports show phase number, step name, error excerpt
- Suggested fixes shown for common error patterns
- Full log path and resume command included
- JSON structured log for tooling

## Dependencies
- Depends on: EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (for state tracking)

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Error tracking, try_step wrapper, report_failure
- scripts/lib/errors.sh (new): Error patterns database

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 3","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:40:42.022477-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:40:42.022477-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:31.562865-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive).

Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope:
- orchestration/state/error-reporting/preflight remains install.sh + scripts/lib owned
- generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-g29","title":"Fix shellcheck + typecheck for generator outputs","description":"ShellCheck and TS type-check were failing due to generator using import.meta.path, and generated scripts triggering SC1091/SC2034. Fix generator, logging shim, and generated scripts so CI passes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:22.156507-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:58.659374-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:58.659374-05:00","close_reason":"Fix generator type errors + make generated scripts ShellCheck-clean (SC1091/SC2034)","labels":["ci","manifest"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","title":"Implement AI agent updates (claude, codex, gemini)","description":"## What
Implement the 'agents' category for acfs-update:
- Claude Code: claude update (built-in) or reinstall via official installer
- Codex CLI: bun update -g @openai/codex
- Gemini CLI: bun update -g @google/gemini-cli

## Technical Details
- Claude Code has native update: ~/.local/bin/claude update
- If claude update fails, fall back to reinstall via installer
- Codex/Gemini are bun global packages - simple bun update

## Considerations
- Claude update may require re-authentication after major updates
- Codex/Gemini updates are quick (just npm package updates)
- Must use target user's bun, not root's

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All agents update correctly
- [ ] Fallback to reinstall works for Claude
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] Authentication state preserved where possible","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:12.067763-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:12.067763-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.187073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hi7","title":"Review misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts and integrate relevant automation","description":"## Goal
Review `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts` (ignore the moonlight tip) and fold the best practices into ACFS in a way that is:
- automated where safe (installer defaults / ~/.acfs assets)
- documented where automation isn’t appropriate

## Scope ideas (examples)
- shell ergonomics that help agent workflows
- git safety patterns (non-destructive defaults, guardrails)
- tmux/terminal workflows

## Constraints
- Do not add “file sprawl”; integrate into existing modules/assets when possible.
- Do not introduce external-contributor posture.

## Deliverables
- A short decision log of what was adopted vs rejected (and why).
- Concrete changes in installer/assets/docs where appropriate.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least 3 high-value tips integrated (excluding moonlight).
- No breaking changes to default install flow.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hii","title":"Create VERSION file and version management","description":"# Task: Create VERSION File and Version Management

## Description
Set up version management for ACFS releases.

## VERSION File
Root of repo: `VERSION`
```
0.1.0
```

## Version Format
Semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to installer
- MINOR: New features
- PATCH: Bug fixes

## Version Usage

### In installer
```bash
ACFS_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL .../VERSION)
```

### In acfs doctor
```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
```

### In website
Show version in footer

### In manifest
```yaml
version: 1
acfs_version: 0.1.0
```

## Release Process
1. Update VERSION file
2. Update CHANGELOG.md
3. Tag release: `git tag v0.1.0`
4. Push: `git push --tags`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VERSION file created
- [ ] All components read version correctly
- [ ] Version shown in doctor output
- [ ] Version shown on website","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:50.115466-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","close_reason":"VERSION file exists with 0.1.0","labels":["infrastructure","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ixt","title":"Fix set -e crash in onboard status progress bar","description":"`onboard status` used `seq 1 0` in command substitution under `set -euo pipefail`, which can cause the script to exit when progress is 0. Replace with safe loops; also make common counter increments `+= 1` so scripts remain safe when sourced under set -e.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:04:33.527609-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:07:15.994218-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:07:15.994218-05:00","close_reason":"Fix onboard status progress bar set -e crash (seq 1 0) by using safe loops; also swap remaining ++ counters to += 1 for set -e safety.","labels":["onboard","reliability"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","title":"Update README with new features documentation","description":"# Task: Update README with new features documentation

## Context
All epics need user-facing documentation in the existing README.

## Features to Document

### 1. Resume Capability
- Explain that install now resumes automatically
- --force-reinstall flag for fresh install
- Silent resume by default (no prompts)

### 2. Pre-Flight Check
- Optional `./scripts/preflight.sh` command
- What it checks (disk, memory, network, apt locks)
- How to interpret pass/warn/fail output

### 3. Better Error Messages
- Explain structured error output format
- Where to find logs
- Common fixes in ERROR_PATTERNS

### 4. Checksum Recovery
- Batched mismatch handling
- CRITICAL vs RECOMMENDED tool behavior
- Manual install for skipped tools

### 5. Enhanced Doctor
- `acfs doctor --deep` flag
- What functional tests check (auth, databases)
- Intentional skip vs missing tool distinction

## Sections to Update (NOT add new)
- Quick Install → mention resume behavior
- Troubleshooting → error patterns, preflight
- Doctor section → --deep flag, output format

## What NOT to Add
- Contributing section (AGENTS.md: "Remove any mention of contributing/contributors")
- Separate new sections for each feature (integrate into existing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All new features documented
- [ ] Examples provided
- [ ] Integrated into existing sections
- [ ] No new "Contributing" section
- [ ] Concise - users should READ the README, not skim it

## Files to Modify
- README.md (only)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:56.158321-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:02:50.429944-05:00","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","author":"jemanuel","text":"Heads up: manifest-driven installer epic  will add new user-facing flags () and recommend curl|bash installs pin to tags/SHA via archive bootstrap. Coordinate README edits with this.","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:08Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","author":"jemanuel","text":"Heads up: manifest-driven installer epic agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt will add user-facing flags (--only/--skip/--print-plan) and recommend curl|bash installs pin to tags/SHA via archive bootstrap. Coordinate README edits with this.","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.1","title":"Add Mermaid architecture diagram to README","description":"## Goal
Add a GitHub-rendering Mermaid diagram to `README.md` that explains ACFS at a glance.

## Diagram requirements
- Must render on github.com (```mermaid fenced block).
- Show the major components and how they connect:
  - `apps/web` (Next.js wizard; no backend; state via URL/localStorage)
  - `install.sh` orchestrator
  - `scripts/lib/*` shared libs (logging/security/state/etc)
  - `acfs.manifest.yaml` (single source of truth)
  - `packages/manifest` generator (produces `scripts/generated/*`)
  - `scripts/generated/*` (module installers + `manifest_index` + doctor checks)
  - `acfs/` assets copied to `~/.acfs/`
  - `acfs doctor` (current + future generated checks)

## Acceptance criteria
- `README.md` includes the diagram in an existing section (no new big section sprawl).
- Diagram matches the real flow: manifest → generator → generated scripts, and `install.sh` sources libs + generated scripts.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:45.373642-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:45.373642-05:00","labels":["docs","readme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:45.379171-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.2","title":"Document Vault secrets posture (install + usage)","description":"## Goal
Document that ACFS includes HashiCorp Vault for secrets management, and explain the intended “default beginner” posture vs advanced usage.

## Why
- Users need a consistent story for secrets (agent keys, DB creds, API tokens).
- Vault is listed as part of the stack; README should make it explicit and non-confusing.

## Scope
- README only (integrate into existing sections).
- Clarify whether Vault is installed by default vs opt-in.
- Link the idea of “secrets management” to the broader ACFS goals (reproducible infra + agent workflow).

## Acceptance criteria
- README explicitly mentions Vault as the recommended secrets tool.
- README clarifies installation default (enabled-by-default vs opt-in) and how to verify it’s present.
- README avoids implying external contributions or PR workflow.

+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.3","title":"Clarify Supabase IPv4 limitation + pooler usage","description":"## Goal
Add a clear warning/guide in `README.md` about Supabase connectivity from a VPS:
- Unless you pay for Supabase features that provide direct IPv4 access, you may not be able to connect directly to your Postgres instance over IPv4.
- In that case you must use the public Supabase pooler (and configure apps accordingly).

## Why
This is a common “it works locally but not on the VPS” failure mode, and beginners will get stuck.

## Acceptance criteria
- README includes a concise, accurate note in an existing DB/Supabase section.
- Mentions the recommended workaround (public pooler) and the practical implication (connection string/host/port differences).

+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.4","title":"Add README guidance: prefer native Claude Code install/update","description":"## Goal
Document the recommended way to install and update Claude Code in ACFS.

## Key points to clarify
- ACFS should prefer the official/native Claude Code installation method (not a random package manager build).
- Updates should use Claude Code’s native update mechanism when available; fall back to reinstall only if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- README mentions the preferred install/update pathway for Claude Code.
- Avoids confusing users with multiple competing install methods.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwa","title":"Fail-fast remote curl|bash invocations in install.sh","description":"Several remote installer invocations in install.sh run via subshells without pipefail (e.g., oh-my-zsh uses sh -c with curl output, and multiple run_as_target bash -c "curl ... | sh"). If curl fails, the subshell can still return success and the installer continues. Fix by running these under bash with set -euo pipefail and passing args safely, so download/exec failures abort correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:17:16.089137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","close_reason":"Wrap remote installer subshells with set -euo pipefail so curl failures abort; also make stack remote installer helper pass args safely and avoid masking download errors.","labels":["installer","reliability"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
echo "Synced command_palette.md"
```

### Install Location
Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Onboard Integration
In Mission 6, show the palette using:
- bat (pretty) if available
- less as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sync script works
- [ ] Palette included in installer
- [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6
- [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","close_reason":"Vendored NTM command_palette.md to acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/. Added sync script scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh with --check mode. Commit 2a09af8.","labels":["onboard","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l2w","title":"Harden sync_ntm_palette.sh: HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing","description":"scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh currently uses plain curl -fsSL and may fetch the remote file twice when computing the hash. Align with scripts/lib/security.sh (HTTPS-only curl args + enforce_https) and compute remote hash from a single fetch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:46:02.630169-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: sync_ntm_palette.sh now uses scripts/lib/security.sh for HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing (commit d683f1f). Shellcheck clean.","labels":["scripts","security"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+name: Monitor Upstream Checksums
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: "0 6 * * *"   # 6 AM UTC daily
+    - cron: "0 18 * * *"  # 6 PM UTC daily (stability check)
+  workflow_dispatch:       # Manual trigger for testing
+
+jobs:
+  check-checksums:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: write
+      pull-requests: write
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Restore checksum cache
+        uses: actions/cache@v4
+        with:
+          path: .checksum-cache
+          key: checksum-monitor-${{ github.run_id }}
+          restore-keys: |
+            checksum-monitor-
+
+      - name: Verify current checksums
+        id: verify
+        run: |
+          chmod +x ./scripts/lib/security.sh
+          ./scripts/lib/security.sh --verify --json > current.json
+
+          # Extract counts from JSON
+          mismatches=$(jq '.mismatches | length' current.json)
+          errors=$(jq '.errors | length' current.json)
+          total_issues=$((mismatches + errors))
+
+          echo "mismatches=$mismatches" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "errors=$errors" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "total_issues=$total_issues" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+
+          if [[ "$total_issues" -gt 0 ]]; then
+            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            jq -r '.mismatches[].name' current.json > changed_tools.txt 2>/dev/null || true
+            jq -r '.errors[].name' current.json >> changed_tools.txt 2>/dev/null || true
+            echo "Changed/errored tools:"
+            cat changed_tools.txt
+          else
+            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "All checksums match!"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Check stability
+        if: steps.verify.outputs.changed == 'true'
+        id: stability
+        run: |
+          # Get current mismatches as sorted list
+          current_issues=$(jq -r '[.mismatches[].name, .errors[].name] | sort | join(",")' current.json)
+
+          if [[ -f ".checksum-cache/previous_issues.txt" ]]; then
+            previous_issues=$(cat .checksum-cache/previous_issues.txt)
+
+            if [[ "$current_issues" == "$previous_issues" ]]; then
+              echo "stable=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+              echo "Checksums stable across 2 runs - safe to create PR"
+            else
+              echo "stable=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+              echo "Issues changed since last run - waiting for stability"
+              echo "Previous: $previous_issues"
+              echo "Current: $current_issues"
+            fi
+          else
+            echo "stable=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "First run with changes - caching for stability check"
+          fi
+
+          # Update cache
+          mkdir -p .checksum-cache
+          echo "$current_issues" > .checksum-cache/previous_issues.txt
+          cp current.json .checksum-cache/previous.json
+
+      - name: Generate updated checksums
+        if: steps.verify.outputs.changed == 'true' && steps.stability.outputs.stable == 'true'
+        run: |
+          ./scripts/lib/security.sh --update-checksums > checksums.yaml.new
+          mv checksums.yaml.new checksums.yaml
+
+      - name: Create Pull Request
+        if: steps.verify.outputs.changed == 'true' && steps.stability.outputs.stable == 'true'
+        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v5
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          title: "chore(security): update upstream checksums"
+          body: |
+            ## Upstream Checksum Updates
+
+            The following installer checksums have changed:
+
+            ```
+            ${{ steps.verify.outputs.mismatches }} mismatches
+            ${{ steps.verify.outputs.errors }} errors
+            ```
+
+            **Changed tools:**
+            $(cat changed_tools.txt | sed 's/^/- /' || echo "- See verification output")
+
+            ### Stability Verified
+
+            Checksums have been stable across 2 consecutive checks (12 hours apart).
+
+            ### Action Required
+
+            Please review the upstream changes before merging. The changes may be due to:
+            - Normal upstream updates (most common)
+            - Security patches
+            - Potential supply chain issues (rare, but verify)
+
+            ---
+            Generated by checksum monitoring workflow
+          branch: auto/update-checksums
+          labels: security, automated
+          delete-branch: true
+
+      - name: Summary
+        run: |
+          echo "## Checksum Monitoring Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Metric | Value |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "|--------|-------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Mismatches | ${{ steps.verify.outputs.mismatches }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Errors | ${{ steps.verify.outputs.errors }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Changed | ${{ steps.verify.outputs.changed }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          if [[ "${{ steps.verify.outputs.changed }}" == "true" ]]; then
+            echo "| Stable | ${{ steps.stability.outputs.stable || 'N/A' }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          fi
